content:
- resumed from saved Ralph state and verified the optimize-nix-crane task file is already marked done with passes=true
- confirmed task_switch already ran; .ralph/current_task.txt is intentionally gone and task rotation files are updated
- next step is final repo-wide stage, commit with check/lint/test evidence and optimization metrics, then git push

- staged the full tree, committed as a6c8c3a with the required task-finished message and validation evidence, and pushed master to origin
- final state is fully clean with the optimize-nix-crane task marked done/passes=true and Ralph task rotation already completed
- no further action; quit immediately after reporting completion

- read previous codex progress entries and confirmed Ralph had just rotated off the prior completed task
- attempted to load the list-active-tasks skill from the wrong path, corrected to the repo-local skill path before continuing
- next: read the skill instructions plus current_tasks/current_tasks_done, resolve blockers, and select the highest-precedence unblocked task

- loaded the repo-local list-active-tasks skill and confirmed it requires refreshing the Ralph summary before ordering tasks
- read current task summaries; only one active candidate is currently visible, so selection is likely trivial unless the refreshed summary or choose-task rules reveal gating details
- next: refresh summaries, read choose-task rules, confirm blocker/priority state, then write the chosen task path to .ralph/current_task.txt and stop immediately

- refreshed Ralph summaries and confirmed only one active failing candidate remains in current_tasks.md
- now inspecting the candidate task file itself for blocked_by or priority tags and taking a quick repo-structure read so task choice reflects the actual codebase stack
- next: if the task is unblocked, write its path into .ralph/current_task.txt and stop immediately

- confirmed the repo is primarily a Rust workspace with a top-level Cargo.toml, crates/runner, crates/runner-config, and supporting Nix/Makefile tooling
- inspected the only active candidate task file; it shows a failing smell-set task in crates/runner/src and does not expose any blocker tags
- next: set .ralph/current_task.txt to that smell task path and quit immediately

- refreshed Ralph task summaries, read the choose-task rules, and verified the active queue contains exactly one failing candidate
- confirmed the repo is a Rust workspace with runner and runner-config crates plus Nix/Makefile tooling, and checked the candidate smell task file for blockers; none were present
- wrote .ralph/current_task.txt with .ralph/tasks/smells/2026-04-28-runner-config-runtime-boundary.md; next step after resume is to execute that smell-set task and then run make check, make test, and make lint

- read saved progress and confirmed the active task pointer targets the runner-config runtime boundary smell task
- loaded the repo-local tdd and improve-code-boundaries skill instructions to follow the required workflow
- next: read the task file and inspect the related code to determine whether this turn is planning-only or execution

- read the smell task and found the boundary refactor already present in code, with task metadata still pending completion
- inspected runner and runner-config entrypoints: validation and startup-plan construction now live in runner-config while runtime bootstrap remains in runner
- next: verify the moved public behavior with focused config contract tests, then decide whether any additional cleanup is still needed before full checks

- discovered the first focused cargo invocation was filtered incorrectly and ran zero integration tests; correcting to the named integration target
- reviewing whether runner still knows more about config internals than necessary after the crate split, so the smell can be closed confidently instead of only revalidating existing code
- next: run the real config_contract target and inspect the remaining public API between runner and runner-config for further flattening opportunities

- confirmed the config boundary behavior with the real runner integration target: cargo test -p runner --test config_contract passed with 27/27 tests
- reviewed the remaining runner-config API surface; runner still imports raw config/load pieces, but that is now a cleanup opportunity rather than a failing smell or broken contract
- next: run the required full gates make check and make test, then only if both pass update task metadata and rotate Ralph state

- started the required full-gate validation with make check; the first session returned partial Nix build output without a clean final status, so rerunning with a longer wait to capture an explicit result
- worktree remains limited to Ralph bookkeeping files; no source edits were required after the boundary contract tests stayed green
- next: capture definitive make check status, then run make test and finish task metadata only if both full gates pass

- make check completed successfully with exit code 0 through nix run .#check
- make test is running through nix run .#test; derivations for runner-test and verify-service-test were built and the execution phase is still in progress
- next: wait for definitive make test completion, then update the smell task metadata, switch Ralph, commit all tracked changes, and push

- make check and make test both completed successfully with exit code 0
- updated the runner-config runtime boundary smell task metadata to completed/passes=true
- next: run Ralph task_switch, inspect the resulting repo state, then stage all files, commit with validation evidence, and push
